qas.a: The reasonable amount of force necessary to accomplish the task.

qas.a: Security Forces protect, defend, and fight to enable Air Force, Joint, and Coalition missions.

qas.q: What is the definition of the Use of Force?
qas.a: The amount of force necessary to achieve the objective or obtain compliance.
A General Global Threat of Possible terrorist activity exists. Warrants a routine security posture
When there is an increased general threat of possible terrorist activity against personnel or facilities, the nature and extent of which are unpredictable. Capable of being maintained indefinitely.
When an increased or more predictable threat of terrorist activity exists. Prolonged periods may affect operational capability and mil/civ relationships.

When an incident occurs or intel is received indicating that some form of terrorist action or targeting against personnel or facilities is likely. Prolonged implementation may create hardship and affect the activities of the unit and its personnel.

Applies in the immediate area where a terrorist attack has occurred or when intel has been received that terrorist action against a specific location or person is imminent. Measures are not intended to be sustained for an extended duration.

qas.q: What gives Security Forces the authority to apprehend?
qas.a: Rule 302 of the MCM and Article 7b of the UCMJ
